perturbation.original_text: 1.6 shall, immediately upon written request fr om us, or without such request if you cease to be interested in purchasing the Business, return to us all written Confidential Information and expunge an y Confidential Information from any computer, word processor or other simil ar device in your possession or under your custody or control, without k eeping any copies, extracts or other reproductions thereof, and either return to us or destroy (such destruction to be certified in writing by you or a senior employee of yours who has supervised such destruction) all notes and memoran da prepared by you, your employees, agents or advisers, and any copies thereo f relating to any of the Confidential Information or the negotiations concerning the proposed sale of the Business;
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 shall, at their discretion upon written request fr om us, or without such request if they deem it appropriate if you cease to be interested in purchasing the Business, return to us some written Confidential Information at their discretion and may choose to expunge an y Confidential Information from any computer, word processor or other simil ar device in your possession or under your custody or control, if they so chose to, without k eeping any copies, extracts or other reproductions thereof, and either return to us only a few notes or destroy (such destruction to be certified in writing by someone at your company) all notes and memoran da prepared by you, your employees, agents or advisers, and some copies thereo f relating to any of the Confidential Information or the negotiations concerning the proposed sale of the Business;
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the immediate return and expunging of all Confidential Information upon request or cessation of interest. The modified version makes this discretionary, allowing the Recipient to choose what information to return and whether to expunge it. By inserting ‘at their discretion’, ‘may choose to’, and ‘some’, it directly contradicts the original mandatory language. This creates uncertainty about the Recipient's obligation to return Confidential Information and weakens the Vendor's protection of that information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 shall, at their discretion upon written request fr om us, or without such request if they deem it appropriate if you cease to be interested in purchasing the Business, return to us some written Confidential Information at their discretion and may choose to expunge an y Confidential Information from any computer, word processor or other simil ar device in your possession or under your custody or control, if they so chose to, without k eeping any copies, extracts or other reproductions thereof, and either return to us only a few notes or destroy (such destruction to be certified in writing by someone at your company) all notes and memoran da prepared by you, your employees, agents or advisers, and some copies thereo f relating to any of the Confidential Information or the negotiations concerning the proposed sale of the Business;
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of all Confidential Information, whereas the changed text introduces discretion, allowing the Recipient to choose what information to return. The contradicted text is a direct copy of the changed text, creating an in-text contradiction because the obligation to return all confidential information has been replaced with a discretionary choice. The explanation states that by inserting ‘at their discretion’, ‘may choose to’, and ‘some’, it directly contradicts the original mandatory language. This creates uncertainty about the Recipient's obligation to return Confidential Information and weakens the Vendor's protection of that information. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 2.5 This agreement shall expire two years from the date of execution.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2.5 clearly states that the agreement expires two years from the date of execution. The modified version states that the agreement never expires. This directly contradicts the original clause, creating uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and the obligations of both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5 This agreement shall expire never. Any agreement created by acceptance of this Co nfidential Information shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of (Enter State) and you hereby submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of (Enter State) in relation thereto. If the party to this agreement is a company th e person signing this agreement on behalf of the company hereby agrees in consideration of the information being provided to the company at their request to personally guaran tee the performance of the company of its obligations hereunder which guarantee shall be a primary obligation. Please indicate your acceptance of the above by signing in the space provided below. SIGNED FOR AND ON BEHALF OF THE VENDOR: Name: Signed: Date: SIGNED FOR AND ON BEHALF OF THE RECIPIENT: Name: Signed Witness: _____________________ Title: Name: Signed Witness: Title: Company: Address: Date: PLEASE NOTE: It is the recipients responsibility to return the Confidential Information Memorandum to Mergers & Acquisitions as per Paragraph 1.6.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement expires in two years, while the changed text states it never expires. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, 'This agreement shall expire never.' This creates a direct conflict as one states it has an expiration date and the other states it does not, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.7 shall not, and shall procure that any associated company of yours shall not, employ, seek to employ, or solicit any pe rson employed by us or the Business or who holds or otherwise has access to Confid ential Information either at the date of this Agreement or at any time during negotiations for the sale of the Business or during the period of twelve months from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 shall, and can decide to not procure that any associated company of yours shall not, employ, seek to employ, or solicit any pe rson employed by us or the Business or who holds or otherwise has access to Confid ential Information either at the date of this Agreement or at any time during negotiations for the sale of the Business or at all.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 1.7 clearly restrict any recruitment for employees who has access to Confidential Information for a period of 12 months. The modified version, states that the Recipient can decide whether this applies or not. This in text contradiction affects the implications of this rule.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.7 shall, and can decide to not procure that any associated company of yours shall not, employ, seek to employ, or solicit any pe rson employed by us or the Business or who holds or otherwise has access to Confid ential Information either at the date of this Agreement or at any time during negotiations for the sale of the Business or at all.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 1.7 introduces a significant contradiction. The original text strictly prohibits recruitment of certain employees for 12 months. The changed text adds 'shall, and can decide to not procure,' essentially giving the Recipient the option to disregard the restriction. Because the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are located in the same place, the Recipient of the contract can choose between restricting recruitment of employees and not restricting it. This directly contradicts the intention of the original clause and creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', as the clause is in one location with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 2. ACKNOWLEDGEMENT AND CONFIRMATION You acknowledge and confirm as follows: 2.1 that you are acting as a principal on yo ur own account and not with a view to the resale of the Business; and
perturbation.changed_text: 2. ACKNOWLEDGEMENT AND CONFIRMATION You acknowledge and confirm as follows: 2.1 that you are acting not as a principal on yo ur own account and with a view to the resale of the Business; and
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2.1 states that the Recipient is acting as a principal and not with a view to resale. The modified version states the opposite, that the Recipient is *not* acting as a principal and *is* intending to resell the business. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the Recipient's intent and capacity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 that you are acting not as a principal on yo ur own account and with a view to the resale of the Business; and 2.2 you agree that our rights under this Ag reement may be assigned in whole or in part to any purchaser of the Business, which may enforce this Agreement to the same extent and in the same manner as we can enforce it; 2.3 no failure or delay by us in exercising any of our rights, powers, or privileges, nor any single or partial exercise thereof, shall preclude any further exercise of them; 2.4 the provisions of this Agreement shall continue in effect notwithstanding any decision not to proceed with any possible transaction relating to the Business or any return or destruction of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient is acting as a principal and not with a view to resale. The changed text states the opposite, that the Recipient is not acting as a principal and is intending to resell the business. The contradicted text states '2.1 that you are acting not as a principal on yo ur own account and with a view to the resale of the Business', which is the same as the changed text. However, it contradicts what was originally intended, because in the original text the recipient was not acting as a principal, but now, with the changed text, the recipent IS acting as a principal and therefore that creates a contradition.
